summary:
The child exhibits behaviors indicative of non-compliance likely stemming from an authoritative home setting characterized by overprotection rather than genuine care for safety; this creates anxiety leading to avoidance tactics like being evasive and slow-moving under pressure. Agent1 reasoned that these symptoms point towards an "Overprotective Mother" type dynamic within the family unit influencing the child’s responses negatively through excessive control which fosters resistant attitudes among kids who feel constrained without room for autonomy despite intentions behind restrictions possibly rooted more deeply out of concern versus effective communication strategies employed during interactions involving directives given to them directly.
